---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

<!--
# Fig 3i - Percentage of DIV-1 vs DIV-3 polarized neurons whose axons
# were retracted by CK-666.

## What this file does

1. Reads ``CK666_WO_analysis.xlsx`` sheet ``Axon_retraction_count``
   (range A1:F17) — Excel-format per-experiment summary table with
   columns including ``Axon_retracted...5`` (the % retracted).
2. Groups experiments by DIV: DIV-1 + DIV-2 vs DIV-3 + DIV-4.
3. Plots boxplot + jittered points comparing the two DIV groups.
4. Saves ``retracted_axon_percentage_DIV1_DIV3.pdf`` (line 124).

## Manuscript panel

- **Fig 3i** (line 112 of ``Arp3_manuscript 20260501.docx``):
  "Percentage of DIV-1 and DIV-3 polarized neurons of which axons were
  retracted by CK-666. N = 3 experiments of DIV-1 and 6 experiments
  of DIV-3 neurons treated with CK-666. *p < 0.05 by Two-tailed t-test."

Body matches the legend exactly:
- N=3 DIV-1 + N=6 DIV-3 experiments → matches the per-experiment xlsx rows.
- Two-tailed t-test → matches ``method = "t.test"`` in body.
- Output ``retracted_axon_percentage_DIV1_DIV3.pdf`` directly visualizes
  the published comparison.

Source Data sheet: ``Fig 3i``.

## Leftover code chunks (no panel use)

The file body contains a leftover chunk (line 137) referencing
``df_summary`` with an antero-vs-retro direction comparison. That data
frame is NOT created earlier in this file — the chunk is **dead code**
inherited from the original ``wave_count.Rmd`` template (the body was
created by copying the wave_count template and substituting the axon
retraction analysis). Knitting will skip the chunk (df_summary not
defined) or it would need separate data.

ED Fig 4 panels (line 334+ of docx) describe different analyses
(growth cone size, neurite extension/retraction duration/velocity
under CK-666, washout) that are NOT in this file's body.

## Original G:\ source

G:/Figure 3_Arp3/Fig. 3i/R_scripts/wave_count.Rmd
(Filename ``wave_count.Rmd`` is the misnomer — the body counts axon
retraction events under CK-666 treatment by DIV, NOT actin waves.)
-->

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
library(pracma)
library(peakPick)
library(quantmod)
library(ggpmisc)
library(tidyverse)
library(magrittr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggfortify)
library(ggpubr)
library(forecast)
library(tsibble)
library(broom)
library(tidyquant)
library(tsbox)
library("pheatmap")
library("RColorBrewer")
library(ggpmisc)
library(ggfortify)
library(viridis) # Default Color Maps from 'matplotlib' 
library(hrbrthemes)
library(data.table)
library(superheat)
library(ggpubr)
```

## load existing/saved environment (.RData)
```{r}
Path_1 <- dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path)
setwd(Path_1)
saved_path <- paste(Path_1, "retracted_axon.RData",  sep = "/")
load(file=saved_path)
Path_1 <- dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path)
```

# save environment (.RData)
```{r}
Path_1 <- dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path)
saved_path <- paste(Path_1, "retracted_axon.RData",  sep = "/")
save.image(file=saved_path)
```

# filter for analyzed cells and relevant columns 
```{r}
library(readxl)
CK666_WO <- read_excel("CK666_WO_analysis.xlsx", sheet = "Axon_retraction_count", range = "A1:F17") %>% as.data.table()

CK666_WO$group <- "DIV1_DIV2"
CK666_WO[...2 == "DIV3"|...2 == "DIV4"]$group <- "DIV3_DIV4"

```

```{r message = FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=10}

my_comparisons <- list(c("DIV1_DIV2", "DIV3_DIV4"))
                       
library(colorspace)
CK666_WO[...2 == "DIV1" | ...2 == "DIV3"]  %>% 
ggplot(aes(x=group, y=Axon_retracted...5, fill = group)) +
  #geom_point()
  theme_classic()+
 # geom_violin(trim=FALSE, kernel ="gaussian", adjust=1)+
  geom_boxplot(width=0.4, fill="white",color="black",alpha=1, outliers = F) +
  geom_jitter(alpha=1, width=0.1, size=5) + 
 # scale_color_continuous_sequential(palette = "Heat", begin = 0.3, end = 1)+ 
# scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0),  limits = c(0, 1.5), breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 10))+ 
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, test = "t.test", paired=F)+
  theme_ipsum(grid = F, ticks = T) +
 theme_linedraw()+
   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), 
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right",
        axis.text=element_text(size=12, face="bold")) #for centering the text 

#plotly::ggplotly(p)
```

```{r}
ggsave(
  "retracted_axon_percentage_DIV1_DIV3.pdf",
   width=7, height=14, 
  plot = last_plot(),
  device = "pdf")
#dev.off()
```

```{r}
Path_1 <- dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path)
saved_path <- paste(Path_1, "wave.RData",  sep = "/")
save.image(file=saved_path)
```
